text:
Cash flow is not a vibe. It is a number.

A lot of new investors estimate rent, guess at expenses, and assume the mortgage payment is manageable. That is not underwriting. That is hope.

Here is how to actually check it:

Gross Monthly Rent
– Vacancy allowance (8–10%)
– Operating expenses: taxes, insurance, management, repairs (~35–45% of gross)
– Debt service (PITI)
= Net Cash Flow

If that number is positive after every deduction, you have a real asset. If it is not, you need to know that before you close — not after.

Run your next rental through the Deal Analyzer before you make the offer. Link in bio.

visual.headline: CASH FLOW IS A NUMBER

text:
The 70% rule is a starting point — not a complete underwrite.

Save this framework before your next walkthrough. Swipe through to see what the rule gets right, what it misses, and how to tighten your analysis before you make any offer.

When you are ready to run the full numbers, use the Deal Analyzer. Link in bio.

text:
Most investors run numbers alone. That is where the expensive mistakes happen.

The Lab is a private community where deals get pressure-tested before you commit time, money, and reputation.

Inside, you get:
– Access to the Deal Analyzer and deal frameworks
– Weekly Deal Breakdown sessions
– A room of operators who will tell you when a deal does not work — before you find out the hard way
– Direct access to Nathaniel for deal feedback and guidance

It is not a course library. It is active deal-room support.

If you are serious about finding and closing real opportunities, this is the next step.

Join the Lab — link in bio.

It is free. It takes 10 minutes. It will tell you whether the deal works before you risk anything.
